texto: The world isn’t perfect, and some days it wears you down. You can either accept that, and face it, and be a help to others instead of a hindrance. Or you can decide the rules are too tough and they shouldn’t apply to you, and you can ignore them and make things harder for everybody else. Sometimes life is about being sad and doing things anyway. Sometimes it’s about being hurt and doing things anyway. The point isn’t perfection. The point is doing it anyway.

texto: The world has a lot to offer. There’s a lot to explore. I like to take advantage of that.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Chloe Neill: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The world has a lot to offer. There’s a lot to explore. I like to take advantage of that.” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, chloe, deep pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de deep e drink.

frase_original: The world has a lot to offer. There’s a lot to explore. I like to take advantage of that.

texto: I wanted what most people wanted—love, companionship.I wanted someone to touch. I wanted someone to touch me back.I wanted someone to laugh with, someone who would laugh with me, laugh at me.I wanted someone who looked and sawme . Not my power, not my position.I wanted someone to say my name. To call out, “Merit,” when it was time to go, or when we arrived.Someone who wanted to say to someone else, with pride, “I’m here with her. With Merit.”I wanted all those things. Indivisibly.But I didn’t want them from Morgan.

seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Chloe Neill sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “I wanted what most people wanted—love, companionship.I wanted someone to touch. I wanted some…

frase_original: I wanted what most people wanted—love, companionship.I wanted someone to touch. I wanted someone to touch me back.I wanted someone to laugh with, someone who would laugh with me, laugh at me.I wanted someone who looked and sawme . Not my power, not my position.I wanted someone to say my name. To call out, “Merit,” when it was time to go, or when we arrived.Someone who wanted to say to someone else, with pride, “I’m here with her. With Merit.”I wanted all those things. Indivisibly.But I didn’t want them from Morgan.
